paper_evaluations.title: Safety and effectiveness of surgical fixation versus non-surgical methods for the treatment of flail chest in adult populations: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

1.reason: The title is not shown; it does not explicitly identify the report as a systematic review in the provided text

2.reason: Structured abstract provided but lacks key PRISMA items (registration, detailed information sources, risk of bias, funding)

3.reason: Rationale and knowledge gap are clearly described in the Introduction

4.reason: Objectives are explicitly stated in Abstract and Methods

5.reason: Inclusion/exclusion criteria reported, but no description of how studies were grouped for syntheses

9.reason: Data collection process lacks details on number of reviewers, independence, and procedures for missing data

13a.reason: No methods described for deciding which studies were eligible for each synthesis

13b.reason: No description of data preparation, conversions, or handling of missing statistics

13e.reason: No methods to explore heterogeneity (e.g., subgroup/meta‑regression) are described

13f.reason: No sensitivity analysis methods are described

14.reason: No methods reported to assess reporting bias (e.g., funnel plots or small‑study effects)

15.reason: No certainty/confidence (e.g., GRADE) assessment methods reported

16a.reason: Search and selection numbers are reported with a PRISMA flow diagram

16b.reason: Excluded studies that appeared eligible and reasons for exclusion are not listed

17.reason: Included studies are cited with characteristics summarized; Supplementary Table 2 referenced

18.reason: Risk of bias assessments presented with figures and narrative

19.reason: Individual study results shown in tables; forest plots display effect estimates with CIs

20a.reason: Syntheses lack summaries of contributing studies’ characteristics and risk of bias per synthesis

20b.reason: Statistical syntheses presented with summary estimates, CIs, and heterogeneity (I2, Tau2) via forest plots/abstract

20c.reason: No investigations of causes of heterogeneity are reported

21.reason: No assessment of risk of bias due to missing results is presented

22.reason: Certainty of evidence assessments are not presented

23a.reason: Results interpreted and compared with prior retrospective studies and meta‑analyses

23b.reason: Limitations of included evidence (small samples, heterogeneity) are discussed

23d.reason: Implications for practice and need for future research are discussed

paper_evaluations.title: Effectiveness of Mechanical Chest Compression Devices over Manual Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation: A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis and Trial Sequential Analysis.

20b.reason: Meta-analysis results include summary estimates, CIs, P-values, and heterogeneity (I2, Q).

21.reason: Publication bias assessments (funnel plot, Egger’s, fail-safe N) are reported.

22.reason: No certainty-of-evidence (e.g., GRADE) results are presented.

Inclusion criteria (population, interventions/comparator, outcomes, languages, study types) are stated
Information sources (PubMed, Embase, Cochrane) and search date (15 July 2022) reported; other sources noted
Full search strategies for all databases are provided in Appendix 1
Two independent reviewers screened titles/abstracts and full texts; Rayyan used
Data extraction variables listed, but number of extractors and independence of extraction not reported
Outcomes (complications up to 1 year; OMAS/AAOS) and time points with MCID are defined
Other data items (study/patient characteristics, orthosis type, diagnostics, weight-bearing) are specified
Risk of bias assessed with MINORS by two reviewers; disagreements resolved by a third
Effect measures specified (OR for dichotomous, MD for continuous) with 95% CIs
Studies contributing to syntheses defined (≥2 studies per outcome); predefined OMAS time points
Methods to estimate missing SDs and convert units (from range/SE/CI) are detailed
Use of RevMan for tabular and graphical displays (tables/figures) described
Random-effects meta-analyses, Mantel–Haenszel/inverse variance, heterogeneity measures, and alpha specified
No methods to explore heterogeneity causes (e.g., subgroup analyses, meta-regression) are described
No sensitivity analysis methods are reported
No methods to assess reporting bias (e.g., funnel plots/Egger’s test) are reported
No certainty of evidence assessment (e.g., GRADE) is reported
Numbers identified, screened, assessed, and included are reported with a PRISMA flow diagram
No list of excluded full-text studies with reasons is provided
Study characteristics (design, sample size, demographics, interventions, classifications) are tabulated
Risk of bias results (MINORS scores per study) are presented in a table
Individual study results (complications and PROMs) are tabulated; figures referenced
No summary of characteristics and risk of bias of studies contributing to each synthesis is provided
Summary effects and CIs reported, but heterogeneity statistics (e.g., I2) are not presented
No results of investigations into heterogeneity causes are reported
No sensitivity analysis results are reported
No assessment of risk of bias due to missing results (reporting bias) is presented
No certainty-of-evidence (e.g., GRADE) assessments are presented
Findings are interpreted and compared with previous literature
Limitations of the evidence (heterogeneity, small numbers, variable follow-up) are discussed
Limitations of the review processes (e.g., screening/extraction methods) are not discussed
Clinical implications and future research needs are discussed
No registration information or statement of non-registration is provided
States that no published review protocol exists
No protocol amendments are reported
Sources of financial/non-financial support and funders’ roles are not reported
Conflict of interest statement declares no conflicts
No statements on availability of data, code, or materials are provided
Title identifies it as a systematic review and meta-analysis.
Explicit aim to compare removable orthosis versus cast for safety and functional outcomes.
Eligibility criteria are not explicitly detailed; only study designs and target condition are mentioned.
No databases, registers, or search dates are reported.
States methodological quality was assessed using the MINORS instrument.
States that a meta-analysis was performed and outcomes synthesized using effect measures (OR, MD).
Reports five studies, their designs (2 RCTs, 3 observational), and total participants (n=516).
Provides summary estimates and CIs but no heterogeneity measures or numbers of studies per outcome.
No limitations of the included evidence are mentioned.
Offers an overall interpretation and implications (orthosis safe, non-inferior outcomes).
No registration information is provided.
Funding sources are not reported.

comparison_details.title: Clinical assessment and risk stratification for prehospital use of methoxyflurane versus standard analgesia in adult patients with trauma pain.

items.ai_reason: Title does not identify the report as a systematic review/meta-analysis
Rationale and context for the review are clearly described
Objectives are explicitly stated with PICO elements
Inclusion/exclusion criteria, populations, comparators, language, and timeframe are specified
Databases are listed but no date of last search is reported
Full, reproducible search strategies for each database are not provided
Selection process lacks explicit independent duplicate screening and details on automation tools
Data extraction methods, number of extractors, independence, and author contact are not described
Outcomes are listed but decision rules for multiple measures/time points are not specified
Other variables sought and assumptions for missing/unclear information are not reported
Risk-of-bias tool stated, but reviewer numbers and independence are not reported
Effect measures (RR/OR, mean difference, 95% CI) are specified
No methods described for determining which studies/results were included in each synthesis
No methods for data preparation or handling missing/heterogeneous data are described
Authors state data were tabulated and results presented in tables/figures
Meta-analysis models, heterogeneity assessment, and software (RevMan) are described
No planned methods to explore heterogeneity (e.g., subgroup or meta-regression) are reported
No sensitivity analyses are described
No methods to assess reporting bias/publication bias are reported
No certainty of evidence assessment (e.g., GRADE) is reported
Numbers at each selection stage are reported and a PRISMA flow diagram is referenced
Excluded studies are not individually cited with specific reasons
Key characteristics of included studies are summarized in text/tables
Risk-of-bias assessments are presented (Figure 1 summary)
Individual study results with group data and effect estimates are provided (Table 1)
Synthesis results do not summarise risk of bias among contributing studies

No results of investigations of heterogeneity are presented
No sensitivity analysis results are presented
No assessment of reporting biases is presented
No assessment of certainty/quality of evidence is presented
Findings are interpreted in the context of prior studies
Limitations of the included evidence (few trials, regional concentration) are discussed
Limitations of the review processes are not explicitly discussed
Implications for practice and future research are discussed
No registration or registry number is reported
No protocol is mentioned or made accessible

Funding is reported as none

Title does not identify the report as a systematic review or meta-analysis.
Objectives are explicitly stated for assessing clinical implications and risk stratification.
Explicit inclusion/exclusion criteria are not reported.
Information sources and last search date are not specified.

comparison_details.title: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis Comparing the Safety and Efficacy of Spinal Anesthesia and Spinal Anesthesia Combined with Obturator Nerve Block in Transurethral Resection of Bladder Tumors.

items.ai_reason: Title states “A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis”
Rationale explains clinical issue and need to evaluate ONB+SA vs SA
Objective to evaluate safety/efficacy of ONB+SA in TURBT is stated
Explicit inclusion/exclusion criteria for study type, participants, interventions, outcomes, languages
Databases and 2000–2021 coverage reported, but no explicit last search dates for each source
Only brief keywords given; full reproducible search strategies per database not reported
Selection process does not explicitly state independent duplicate screening of records/reports
Data extraction by two researchers reported, but independence and author contact not described
All outcomes sought are listed (ONR, perforation, LOS, recurrence)
Other variables listed, but no assumptions for missing/unclear data are described
Tools (Cochrane domains, Jadad) reported, but reviewer numbers/independence not stated
Effect measures (RR, MD with 95% CI) specified
No explicit process describing how studies/results were assigned to each synthesis
Data preparation reported (e.g., unit conversion for length of stay)
Forest plots used to display individual study and pooled results
Synthesis methods detailed (fixed/random effects, I2/Q thresholds, models)
Heterogeneity explored via subgroup analyses (inguinal vs pubic approach)
Leave-one-out sensitivity analysis described and presented
Publication bias assessed using a funnel plot
No certainty of evidence assessment (e.g., GRADE) reported
Flow diagram referenced; 444 records screened, 8 studies included
No list of excluded studies with reasons
Study characteristics tabulated (Table 1) with citations
Risk-of-bias judgments for each study presented (Table 2)
Individual study results shown in forest plots with effect estimates and event counts
No synthesis-level summary of contributing studies’ characteristics and risk of bias
Pooled estimates with 95% CIs and heterogeneity statistics reported for all outcomes
Results of subgroup analyses to explore heterogeneity are reported
Sensitivity analysis results (leave-one-out) reported
Reporting bias assessment (funnel plot) and interpretation provided
Certainty of evidence across outcomes not assessed or reported
Findings interpreted in context of prior studies and clinical rationale
Limitations of included evidence discussed (few studies, bias concerns)
Limitations of the review processes (e.g., search limits, registration) not discussed
Implications for practice and need for further research stated
No review registration or protocol information reported
No protocol access location provided
No protocol amendments reported
No sources of financial or non-financial support reported
Competing interests statement provided
Only ‘data available on request’; no public access to data, code, or materials
Title explicitly identifies this as a systematic review and meta-analysis.
States objective to evaluate ONB+SA during TURBT via systematic review/meta-analysis.
Eligibility criteria not fully specified; only RCT design and timeframe mentioned.
Lists databases searched and timeframe (Jan 2000–Dec 2021).
Risk of bias assessed using the Jadad scale.
States meta-analysis (using R) was performed to synthesise results.
Reports 8 included studies and 635 participants; RCTs in TURBT.
Presents pooled effect estimates (RR/MD) with 95% CIs for main outcomes.

Provides a general interpretation and implications of findings.

comparison_details.title: Efficacy of ketorolac in the treatment of acute migraine attack: A systematic review and meta-analysis.

items.ai_reason: Title explicitly states 'systematic review and meta-analysis'.
Introduction explains the rationale for reassessing ketorolac efficacy in acute migraine.
Objective clearly stated: evaluate efficacy of parenteral ketorolac for acute migraine.
Explicit inclusion/exclusion criteria for RCTs, populations, interventions, settings, and language.
Databases, registries, reference lists, and search dates (to Jan 2021) are reported.
Full search strategies (search strings/filters) for each database are not reported.
Two authors independently assessed eligibility; PRISMA flow diagram used.
Data extraction process lacks details on number of extractors and independence/duplication.
Primary and secondary outcomes are listed and defined, with 1-hour timepoint specified.
Other data items (participants, interventions, setting, follow-up) listed; handling of missing data described.
Risk-of-bias domains specified; three authors assessed with disagreements resolved by discussion.
Effect measures specified (RR for dichotomous, SMD for continuous, 95% CIs).
Studies grouped into eight comparator-based syntheses; single-trial analyses reported when applicable.
Data preparation methods described (SMD for differing scales, extraction from figures, cluster adjustments).
No explicit methods for tabulating or visually displaying results were described.
Random-effects meta-analysis in RevMan; heterogeneity assessed with I2 and Cochrane thresholds.
Planned subgroup analyses and sensitivity analyses to explore heterogeneity are described.
Sensitivity analysis methods are described.
Planned funnel plots to assess reporting bias if sufficient studies.
Certainty assessed using GRADE methodology.
Search and selection numbers reported; PRISMA flow diagram presented.
No list of excluded full-text studies with citations and reasons is provided.
Characteristics of included studies presented in a table with key details.
Risk-of-bias assessments for each study presented (figure).
Individual study results shown via forest plots and single-trial effect estimates with CIs.
Per-synthesis summaries of contributing studies’ characteristics and risk of bias not presented.
Synthesis results reported with effect estimates, 95% CIs, p values, and I2.
No results of formal heterogeneity investigations (e.g., subgroup/meta-regression) are presented.
No sensitivity analysis results are reported.
No assessment results for publication bias or small-study effects are reported.
Certainty (GRADE) reported for outcomes in Summary of Findings tables and text.
Results interpreted in context of prior evidence and comparator treatments.
Limitations of included evidence discussed (few trials, small samples, heterogeneity, non-usable data).
Review process limitations discussed (English-only, search limits, extraction from figures).
Implications for practice and future research are discussed.
Registered in PROSPERO with registration number (CRD42021233911).
Protocol location indicated via PROSPERO registration number.
No protocol amendments are described.
Sources of support/funding for the review and funders’ roles are not reported.
Authors’ competing interests are not reported.
Availability of data, code, and materials is not reported.
Title identifies the report as a systematic review and meta-analysis.
Explicit objective to evaluate efficacy of parenteral ketorolac for acute migraine.
Eligibility specified: randomized controlled trials of ketorolac vs other medications in migraine.
Sources and search date reported (CENTRAL, Medline, Google Scholar; up to Jan 2021).
No methods for risk-of-bias assessment are reported.
No synthesis methods (e.g., meta-analysis model) are described.
Thirteen trials and 944 participants reported; key comparators summarized.
Results lack confidence intervals and counts per outcome; precision not reported.
Evidence limitations noted (inadequate number of trials).
General interpretation and implications are provided.
No registration information is provided.
No funding source is reported in the abstract.
